title: ParseLevel fails on whitespace-padded level strings
body: ParseLevel(" info ") returns an error because parseLevel does an exact bytescomparison without trimming surrounding whitespace, which trips us up when the level comes from a config file value that has trailing spaces.

title: Document interaction between JSONFormatter.DataKey and FieldMap
body: When DataKey is set, JSONFormatter nests entry.Data under that key, but it's unclear from the comments in json_formatter.go whether FieldMap-renamed keys (time/level/msg) also get nested under DataKey or stay at the top level.

title: SyslogHook maps FatalLevel to the same severity as PanicLevel
body: SyslogHook.Fire sends both logrus.PanicLevel and logrus.FatalLevel to hook.Writer.Crit, losing the distinction between the two levels once they reach syslog, which makes alerting rules that only want to fire on true panics also fire on fatals.

title: Clarify how terminal_check_windows.go detects redirected output
body: We're seeing color codes appear in a log file when a Windows service redirects logrus's stdout, and it's not obvious from terminal_check_windows.go what API it uses to decide 'isatty' on Windows or why it's returning true for our redirected handle.

title: Writer hook produces duplicate output when combined with default stdout logger
body: Adding hooks/writer/writer.go's hook to a Logger that also has its default Out set to os.Stdout results in each log line appearing twice, since both the base logger output and the hook's writer target the same stream.
